title: Americans Stuck Overseas During War
summary: When a war started between the U.S. and Iran, the U.S. government told Americans to leave some countries quickly. But many airports closed, so thousands of Americans got stuck far from home. Over five days, about 24,000 people returned safely to the U.S. This article shares stories from Americans who were stranded. Emaan Abbass lived in Dubai and found it hard to leave. Colleen West was in Jerusalem with her husband and got back to Utah after the war began. Matt Gentile was in Iraq and returned to New York recently. John Almeida and his wife were in Dubai on vacation and got stuck just before their flight home. They all describe their struggles to return during the war.

keywords.explanation: stuck in a place and cannot leave
when airports are shut down and planes cannot fly
a part of the U.S. government that helps Americans in other countries

questions.question: What did the U.S. government tell Americans to do when the war started?
Why did many Americans get stuck overseas?
How many Americans returned safely in five days?
Where was Emaan Abbass living when the war started?
Who was in Jerusalem with her husband?
What were John Almeida and his wife doing in Dubai?

background_read: This article is about Americans who were in other countries when a war began between the U.S. and Iran. Wars can make travel dangerous, so governments often tell people to leave. Sometimes, airports close during wars, which means planes cannot fly, and people get stranded. This can be scary because they are far from home and family. The stories here show how different people, like those on vacation or living abroad, faced this problem and tried to get back to the U.S. safely.
Article_Structure: WHO: Americans like Emaan Abbass, Colleen West, Matt Gentile, and John Almeida who were overseas
WHAT: They got stranded when a war started and airports closed, making it hard to return home
WHERE: In countries like Dubai, Jerusalem, and Iraq
WHY: Because the U.S. and Iran went to war, leading to travel disruptions

perspectives.description: The article shares individual stories to show how the war affected real people trying to get home.
